summary: March CPI came in at 3.3% annually, the highest since May 2024, driven by the ongoing Middle East conflict's impact on energy prices. FOMC minutes from the March meeting showed policymakers concerned the conflict could sustain inflationary pressures, though they still anticipated one rate cut this year.
tweet: Hot CPI at 3.3% and a fragile US-Iran ceasefire are keeping the Fed on hold and gold elevated while equities and risk assets push higher on relief.
